element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental punch/kick system against starting opponent
Strategic combat tool integrated across story progression
Strategic element used tactically during matches
Climactic confrontation in kidnapping narrative arc
Mysterious antagonist with identity revelation at story conclusion

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Best-of-three match structure with progressive difficulty through story mode stages toward climactic boss encounters

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory opponents to establish fighting mechanics and controls
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive opponent difficulty with increased AI aggression and special move usage
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters (Mr. Big, Mr. Karate) with complex attack patterns and narrative significance
notable_spikes: Mr. Big stage represents significant difficulty jump
Mr. Karate final boss introduces enigmatic fighting style and story cliff-hanger tension

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear progression through kidnapping rescue narrative with opponents thematically tied to storyline (Mac's Bar stage, etc.). Eight characters available in default roster with Mr. Big and Mr. Karate unlockable.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mac's Bar (Jack's stage in JP, renamed for regional content policies)

notable_level_patterns.description: Spirit gauge enhancement mechanics accessed between stages - 'Beer Bottle Cut' (JP) / generic bottle cutting (US/EU) and 'Super Death Blow' / 'Super Fire Blow' (JP/US respectively)
Standard fighting game convention with two consecutive victories required to advance, creating tension/relief cycles within each stage encounter

sources_used: tcrf - Art of Fighting (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
wikipedia - Art of Fighting series article
